metadata.title: How a Genetic Bottleneck Built the Largest Society on Earth
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = cooperation / low diversity / supercolony; CRIMSON = war / high diversity / native range
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no hydrocarbon chemistry, no San Diego inter-supercolony war, no fire ant competition. Wonder register.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: A two-millimeter ant with weak mandibles has built the largest animal society on Earth — spanning six continents — because a genetic accident stripped away the diversity that would have made its own cousins enemies.

card.copy: How a Genetic Bottleneck Built the Largest Society on Earth

beats.narration_text: In South America, where Argentine ants evolved, colonies are genetically diverse and fight constantly. Two workers from colonies 200 meters apart show immediate aggression when placed together. The diversity maintains borders.

production_viz.label: South America map; colony territories with aggressive borders; two workers from 200m apart fighting; CRIMSON conflict

beats.narration_text: A few queens stowed away on cargo ships in the late 19th century. The voyage was a genetic bottleneck: only a tiny fraction of the population's genetic diversity made it to new continents. What arrived was almost genetically uniform.

card.copy: Why do invasive Argentine ants cooperate globally?
card.sub: A genetic bottleneck stripped away the diversity that causes war

beats.narration_text: Ants recognize nestmates using cuticular hydrocarbons — chemical signatures on their outer surface, derived from their genetics. Diverse genetics produce diverse chemical signatures; ants learn their colony's profile and attack anything different.

production_viz.label: Ant cuticle with chemical signature icons; diverse genetics → diverse signatures → aggression (CRIMSON); uniform → cooperation (TEAL)

beats.narration_text: In the invasive range, the bottleneck produced uniformly similar chemical profiles across all colonies. When workers from California and Spain meet, their chemical signatures are too similar to trigger aggression. They cooperate. They groom each other.

production_viz.label: California and Spain workers meeting; chemical signatures similar; grooming behavior; TEAL cooperation

beats.narration_text: The result: a single supercolony spanning California, Spain, Japan, and Australia — colonies separated by oceans for decades, yet cooperative when introduced. Tests of invasive ants from different continents show no aggression. They behave as one colony of billions.

production_viz.label: World map with gold lines linking California-Spain-Japan-Australia as one supercolony; TEAL cooperation network

beats.narration_text: This is paradoxical: the same trait — low diversity — that limits Argentine ant competitiveness at home liberates them everywhere else. They overwhelm native ants not through superior weaponry but through unlimited colony size.

production_viz.label: Low diversity: bad at home (limited adaptation), good invasive (unlimited colony size); paradox diagram; TEAL=invasive advantage

beats.narration_text: The supercolony is not coordinated by any queen. There is no headquarters. It is an emergent property: billions of ants that happen to be chemically too similar to fight, so they cooperate by default. The genetic bottleneck accidentally invented the largest society.

production_viz.label: Emergent network: no central control; local chemical recognition rule → global cooperation; TEAL emergence arrows

beats.narration_text: Climate change is making the supercolony larger. Argentine ants in their native range are limited by cold winters. In invaded regions, their range is expanding poleward. The low-diversity invasion — already on six continents — is still spreading.

production_viz.label: Range map expanding poleward with warming; six continents already; TEAL=current range, arrows=expansion

beats.narration_text: High diversity means diverse chemical signatures means inter-colony warfare means borders maintained. Genetic bottleneck means low diversity means similar signatures means kin-recognition fails means global cooperation. The accident of one cargo shipment built a supercolony.

card.sub: One cargo shipment. Six continents. Billions of ants.

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
